description.value:
An advanced variation of the "Hidden Shadow Shot" you prepare Two Thrown weapons in your hand launching them at the same time feigning that they are aimed at the same person. Your weapons collide mid-air, sending the other one to hit a new target instead.

Select two targets, and make one attack roll targeting both of them. Select one target, and that target must make a Perception check versus your attack roll, on a fail they are unable to notice the incoming switch-up attack, and cannot react to the attack roll. On hit, both enemies take double your [weapons damage die] + 4d6 damage, inflicting 2 ranks of bleed on hit.

If this jutsu is cast as a Chain 2, increase the damage die by +3, if this jutsu is cast as a Chain 3, increase the damage die by an additional +2, and inflicts 2 ranks of lacerated on hit in place of bleed.

At Higher Ranks: For each rank you cast this above A- Rank, increase the cost by 3, the damage by 2d6 and the ranks of bleeding by 2

effects.name: Bleeding

changes.value:
turn=start, 
damageRoll=1d4, damageType=necrotic,
label=Bleeding,
damageBeforeSave=true,
actionSave=dialog,
saveRemove=true,
saveDC=15,
chatFlavor=Lil paper cut fr,
rollType=skill,
saveAbility=med,
fastForwardDamage=true,

effects.description:
Max Ranks: 5

A Bleeding creature has suffered a minor abrasion to their flesh, causing slow blood loss if untreated.

  • At the start of their turn, a bleeding creature takes 1d4 necrotic damage for each rank of Bleed they have.

  • A Bleeding creature cannot gain the benefits of a rest.

  • As an Action, a Bleeding creature may attempt to stop the bleeding by succeeding on a DC 15 Medicine check.

  • A creature who would gain a sixth (or more) ranks of the Bleeding condition immediately gains 5 ranks of the Lacerated condition, ending the Bleeding condition.

    • The first time per rest a Bleeding creature would gain the Lacerated this way, they must succeed a DC 11 flat check, immediately taking the damage of their Lacerated ranks as they suffer a great wound.

  • Outside of combat, every 10-minutes a creature suffers the effects of this condition as if a turn has passed in combat.

effects.name: Lacerated

changes.value:
turn=start,
damageRoll=1d6,
damageType=necrotic,
label=Lacerated,
chatFlavor=THIS A BIG CUT CUH,
saveDC=20,
actionSave=dialog,
rollType=skill,
actionSave=true,
saveAbility=med,
fastForwardDamage=true,

effects.description:
Max Ranks: 100

A Lacerated creature has suffered a major wound on their body and begins to bleed profusely, the rushing blood causing the wound to grow in intensity with each passing second.

  • A Lacerated creature is counted as Bleeding for the purposes of interacting with Features, Traits and Jutsu

  • At the beginning of a Lacerated creatures turn, they take 1d6 necrotic damage per rank of Lacerated they have.

  • A Lacerated creature that gains ranks of Bleeding, gains ranks of Lacerated instead.

  • A Lacerated creature cannot gain the benefits of a rest.

  • As an Action, A Lacerated creature may attempt to stop the Laceration by succeeding on a DC 20 Medicine check with a Medicine Kit.

  • A creature with ranks of lacerated gains one rank of laceration at the end of its turns.

  • A creature immune to the bleeding condition is immune to the lacerated condition.

  • Outside of combat, every 10-minutes a creature suffers the effects of this condition as if a turn has passed in combat.
